Title: CRA Form NS428 and Worksheet NS428 2025 Nova Scotia resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5003-c/5003-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5003-c/5003-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5003-d/5003-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5003-d/5003-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form NS428 2025 calculates Nova Scotia tax for Nova Scotia residents. Part A calculates Nova Scotia tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $30,507, $61,015, $95,883, and $154,650. The rates are 8.79%, 14.95%, 16.67%, 17.5%, and 21%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $2,681.57, $7,242.51, $13,055.01, and $23,339.23.

Part B calculates Nova Scotia non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $11,744. Worksheet NS428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $5,734, starts reducing above $30,828, is zero at $69,055 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $12,618, subtract the greater of $874 and the spouse or dependant net income, and cap the amount at $11,744. Line 58230 multiplies eligible young-child months by $100. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,173. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $7,341 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $1,637 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 8.79%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 8.79% and line 14 by 21%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Nova Scotia non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Nova Scotia tax. Line 55 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet NS428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 1.5% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 8.85%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 57.5%. Line 61 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 14 by 57.5%. Lines 65 to 84 calculate the Nova Scotia low-income tax reduction: the basic reduction is $300, spouse and eligible dependant reductions are $300 each with a $600 subtotal cap, dependent children born in 2007 or later are $165 each, adjusted family income above $15,000 reduces the amount at 5%. Line 86 multiplies Nova Scotia political contributions by 75% and caps the credit at $750. Line 88 multiplies food bank qualifying donations for farmers by 25%. Line 90 multiplies labour-sponsored venture capital share cost by 20% and caps the credit at $2,000. Lines 92 to 97 subtract equity, innovation equity, and venture capital tax credits. Line 98 gives a $1,000 age tax credit if the taxpayer was born in 1959 or earlier and taxable income is less than $24,000. Line 99 enters Nova Scotia tax on line 42800. Normalized numeric values encoded from NS428 and Worksheet NS428 include 30507, 61015, 95883, 154650, 0.0879, 0.1495, 0.1667, 0.175, 0.21, 2681.57, 7242.51, 13055.01, 23339.23, 11744, 5734, 30828, 69055, 0.15, 12618, 874, 100, 1173, 7341, 1637, 0.03, 0.575, 0.015, 0.0885, 300, 600, 165, 15000, 0.05, 750, 0.75, 0.25, 0.20, 2000, 1000, and 24000.
